{"step_by_step_thinking": "Cabotegravir is a long-acting injectable antiretroviral medication classified as an integrase inhibitor. Multiple documents confirm its use in preexposure prophylaxis (PrEP) for HIV prevention. Document [4] states that cabotegravir possesses both preventive potential and therapeutic efficacy. Document [9] explicitly notes that cabotegravir is used in both treatment and prevention. Document [16] and [26] state that in large studies, men, women, and transgender women using cabotegravir had a 69% to 90% lower chance of contracting HIV through sexual transmission compared to those on tenofovir disoproxil fumarate/emtricitabine. Document [19] notes that cabotegravir has efficacy against HIV-2 in vitro, and Document [3] confirms that it is used for the prevention of sexual transmission of HIV across populations. These findings consistently support that cabotegravir is effective for HIV prevention. Therefore, the correct answer is 'yes'.", "answer_choice": "A"}
